articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.headline: Dortmund captain Emre Can out for season with torn ACL
articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

images.name: Can Dortmund torn ACL_260228 [1296x729]
images.caption: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.
images.alt: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.

images.name: Bayern Munich wins 3-2 thriller over Dortmund
images.caption: Bayern Munich gets a late goal from Joshua Kimmich to beat Borussia Dortmund 3-2 in Der Klassiker.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1. Granit Xhaka (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Adam Hlozek.

Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1. Hugo Ekitike (Eintracht Frankfurt) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Farès Chaïbi with a cross   following a corner.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from the right side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Adam Hlozek.
First Half ends, Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3. Exequiel Palacios (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Victor Boniface replaces Patrik Schick.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Piero Hincapié replaces Arthur.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Hugo Larsson replaces Ellyes Skhiri.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Ansgar Knauff replaces Niels Nkounkou.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jonathan Tah replaces Granit Xhaka.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jeremie Frimpong replaces Nathan Tella.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4. Jeremie Frimpong (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Adam Hlozek.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Aurélio Buta replaces Junior Dina Ebimbe.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Jean-Mattéo Bahoya replaces Farès Chaïbi.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Nacho Ferri replaces Hugo Ekitike.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Gustavo Puerta replaces Robert Andrich.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 5. Victor Boniface (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the high centre of the goal.

article.story:
Champions Bayer Leverkusen extended their unbeaten streak as they secured a 5-1 away victory over Eintracht Frankfurt in the Bundesliga on Sunday.

Manager Xabi Alonso, whose side was heavily rotated, had to watch from the stands as he served a touchline ban for yellow card accumulation, but it made no difference as Leverkusen continued their dominant form this season.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

"Our level, our consistency throughout the season is great. But we are always moving forward. We are always concentrating on the work and improving," Alonso said.

The result leaves Leverkusen at the summit with 84 points, while Frankfurt sit sixth with 45. Leverkusen's record unbeaten run now extends to 48 matches across all competitions -- levelling Benfica's 59-year-old record in European football since the introduction of UEFA club competitions.

It was also a record-setting night for Leverkusen goalkeeper Lukas Hradecky, who set a Bundesliga record for the most appearances in goal for a non-German player.

"The curse has finally been broken after five years," Hradeck said of winning at Frankfurt for the first time in his career. "It always felt good to win here with the home team and this time we managed it with the away team.

"The run keeps going, hopefully for another five games. It's simply a joy to play with Bayer at the moment."

Leverkusen broke the deadlock with Granit Xhaka's bending strike from outside the box after 12 minutes, while Frankfurt equalised at the 32nd minute with Hugo Ekitike's perfectly timed header, surpassing Leverkusen's defence.

The visitors could have gone two ahead minutes later but a valiant effort from the Frankfurt defence denied Leverkusen a clear opportunity on an open goal.

Leverkusen regained the lead, though, just before half-time with Patrick Schick's towering header, and secured their third goal in the 58th minute from a penalty converted by Exequiel Palacios after Niels Nkounkou pulled Nathan Tella by the shirt.

Jeremie Frimpong added a fourth for the visitors at the 77th minute, tapping in from short distance a cross into the box while Victor Boniface made it five from the penalty spot a minute before time after Ansgar Knauff tripped Frimpong.

Along with being Bundesliga champions, Leverkusen are still alive in the Europa League semifinals with a second leg against Roma looming Thursday and the finals of the German Cup where they face Kaiserslautern in the final on May 25.

"It's a big challenge, we have two [league] games left, it will be the perfect run. We will certainly try," Alonso said. "We still have games to play to achieve our big goals in all competitions. The next game is important for us, the second leg of the Europa League semifinal: a chance to reach the final.

"In football it's sometimes difficult to stop. You have to prepare for the next game. And the next game is a big game for us. Hopefully we have the chance to be in a European final again."
